title: nepeta parnassica subsp. orphanidea
intro: Nepeta parnassica subsp. orphanidea, also known as the Balkan catmint, is a subspecies of the Nepeta parnassica plant, a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to Greece and Bulgaria. The medicinal properties of Balkan catmint include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory problems, and reduce stress and anxiety, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Balkan catmint can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
